prompt: Clean educational technical infographic for a reverse osmosis lesson, white background, crisp modern engineering style, flat vector-like composition rendered as PNG. Left panel: block 1 is a left water chamber with the exact text label 'Low Solute'; block 2 is a central semipermeable membrane; block 3 is a right chamber with the exact text label 'High Solute'; block 4 is a blue arrow crossing the membrane labeled 'Natural Osmosis'. Right panel: block 1 is a high-pressure feed side with a pressure piston icon pushing inward; block 2 is a membrane; block 3 is a clean-water permeate side; block 4 is a blue arrow crossing the membrane labeled 'Reverse Osmosis'; block 5 is retained contaminants on the feed side. Bottom strip: mini dead-end filtration sketch with water going straight into a filter, next to a cross-flow filtration sketch with feed water moving parallel to the membrane.

Multi-panel educational infographic about water quality metrics for reverse osmosis, clean lab style, flat technical illustration, white background. Panel 1: a digital TDS meter immersed in a glass of water. Panel 2: a conductivity-to-TDS conversion chart with the exact text note '0.5 to 0.7'. Panel 3: SDI test concept with a membrane disc becoming darker. Panel 4: LSI scaling risk shown as calcium scale depositing onto a membrane surface. Panel 5: chlorine symbols feeding into a carbon pre-filter block, then a TFC membrane block with a red warning icon. Make all text labels large and readable.

Clean block diagram of a standard 5-stage domestic reverse osmosis system, technical training style, white background. Diagram from left to right: feed water inlet; sediment filter housing; carbon pre-filter housing; second carbon filter housing; RO membrane housing. From the membrane block, upper branch: a permeate outlet, a check valve, a storage tank, and a faucet. Lower branch: concentrate outlet, flow restrictor, and drain line. Use arrows and the exact text labels 'Feed', 'Permeate', and 'Concentrate' clearly.

Comparison diagram of reverse osmosis pre-filters, educational product-cutaway style, clean white background. Three vertical blocks. Left: a melt-blown polypropylene sediment filter cutaway with trapped dirt and silt. Middle: a granular activated carbon cartridge with irregular carbon granules adsorbing organics. Right: a carbon block cartridge with dense uniform media. Bottom strip: icon for particle capture, icon for chlorine removal, and a highlighted note with the exact text 'Chloramines require Catalytic Carbon'.

Exploded technical diagram of a spiral-wound reverse osmosis membrane, clean engineering illustration, white background. Center: a permeate collection tube. Around it in layers: permeate carrier layer, polyamide thin-film active layer, polysulfone support layer, polyester backing, and feed spacer mesh. Add arrows showing feed water entering, purified permeate moving inward, and concentrate moving outward. Include a small inset showing concentration polarization: a salt boundary layer building up, and turbulence from cross-flow breaking the layer apart.

Process diagram for post-filtration and remineralization after reverse osmosis, educational technical style, white background. Left to right: block 1 is RO permeate with the exact text label 'Low pH'; block 2 is a carbon polishing filter; block 3 is a remineralization cartridge containing media; block 4 is a glass of finished water with the exact text label 'Balanced pH'. Add small chemistry icons for carbon dioxide absorption and buffering restoration.

Cutaway educational diagram of a hydropneumatic reverse osmosis storage tank, white background, modern engineering illustration. Show three tanks as a progression. Left tank: empty tank, small water volume, large air chamber, exact text label '7 to 8 PSI'. Middle tank: partially filled tank, compressed air chamber, rising pressure arrows. Right tank: nearly full tank, highly compressed air chamber, and a note with the exact text 'ASV Closes at 2/3 Line Pressure'. Add the exact text 'Boyle\'s Law: P1V1 = P2V2'.

Split comparison diagram of reverse osmosis pump options, clean technical infographic, white background, blue and orange accents. Left side: feed water line entering an electric booster pump, then a pressure switch, then a membrane housing, with the exact text labels '80 to 100 PSI' and 'Electric Booster'. Right side: brine line driving a hydro-mechanical permeate pump mechanism, permeate line being pushed into the tank, with the exact text label 'Permeate Pump (ERP)'. Show energy and water flow arrows.

Educational infographic about RO waste water and efficiency, white background, clean labels. Top section: feed water splitting into a smaller permeate stream and a larger concentrate stream, labeled with the exact text 'Recovery Ratio: 1 to 4'. Bottom section has three comparison blocks. Left: correctly sized flow restrictor. Middle: restrictor too small causing fouling. Right: restrictor too large causing excess drain flow.

Three-column comparison visual for choosing a reverse osmosis system, clean product infographic on white background. Column 1: standard undersink tanked system with filter housings, membrane, and tank. Column 2: compact tankless direct-flow unit with integrated booster pump. Column 3: simplified icons for countertop and whole-house options. Under each column include badges for output, noise, and electricity. Add a highlighted note for tankless systems with the exact text 'Warning: TDS Creep'.

Workshop flat-lay style instructional illustration of the tools and materials needed for RO installation, white background, clean technical style. Show: tubing cutter, push-to-connect fittings, blue collet clips, adjustable wrench, drill, titanium step bit, PTFE tape, pressure gauge, and coiled RO tubing. Place clean, simple pointing lines to the tools without any text labels.

Under-sink plumbing diagram for installing a domestic reverse osmosis system, simplified for teaching, white background. Top left: cold-water angle stop. Center: mounted pre-filters and membrane. Bottom center: storage tank. Top right: faucet on the sink deck. Bottom right: drain saddle mounted above the P-trap on the vertical tailpiece. Draw tube paths clearly in different colors for feed, permeate, brine, and drain lines.

Step-by-step startup sequence graphic for a new reverse osmosis system, white background, clean infographic style. Block 1: new filters and dry membrane. Block 2: system filling the storage tank, exact text label '2 to 3 hours'. Block 3: faucet opened and dark first water being discarded. Block 4: repeat cycle icon. Add a warning callout with the exact text 'Do Not Consume First Tank'.

Safety-focused reverse osmosis leak testing diagram, white background. Main block: complete RO system at static pressure with highlighted leak points at housings and tubing connections. Side block 1: close-up of a push fitting with a blue collet clip correctly installed. Side block 2: a cabinet floor leak detector shutoff valve using an expanding sponge mechanism.

Instructional figure about handling an RO membrane correctly, technical clean white background. Left: membrane cartridge with the brine seal highlighted. Middle: membrane housing and correct insertion direction arrow. Right: close-up of permeate tube O-rings with silicone grease being applied. Add a red warning symbol over a jar with the exact text 'No Petroleum Jelly'.

Monitoring diagram for inline TDS measurement in an RO system, white background. Block 1: feed water line with inline TDS probe. Block 2: RO membrane housing. Block 3: permeate line with inline TDS probe. Block 4: digital monitor with the exact text labels 'Feed TDS', 'Permeate TDS', and 'Rejection %'. Add a small inset showing a thermistor for temperature compensation.

Maintenance dashboard infographic for reverse osmosis service intervals, white background. Main section: calendar bars for sediment filter, carbon filters, and membrane intervals. Side inset 1: delta-pressure monitoring showing a pressure gauge before and after the pre-filters. Side inset 2: biological loading shown as bacteria risk in an old carbon filter.

Symptom-to-cause diagnostic flowchart for reverse osmosis faults, clean educational infographic on white background. Start row: three symptom boxes with the exact text 'Continuous Drain', 'Rapid Cycling', and 'Low Flow'. Branches lead downward to icons representing a failed check valve, waterlogged tank, and membrane fouling.

Stepwise sanitization protocol diagram for a reverse osmosis system, white background. Sequential blocks: remove all filters; add bleach to the first housing; run system until disinfectant reaches the faucet; sit pressurized for contact time; flush thoroughly. Include a caution panel with the exact text 'Flush Completely Before Use'.

Hardware architecture block diagram for an IoT-enabled reverse osmosis monitor, white background. Feed-line sensor; permeate-line sensor; flow sensor; central ESP32 microcontroller board; Wi-Fi transmission waves; and a smartphone dashboard panel. Show signal arrows connecting the sensors to the microcontroller.

Comparison infographic for advanced reverse osmosis upgrades, clean white background. Left: UV-C sterilizer as the final stage, showing the glowing blue lamp, quartz sleeve, and water path. Right: auto-flush solenoid valve on the brine line, bypassing the restrictor, with a strong flush arrow sweeping across the membrane surface.

Large-scale whole-house reverse osmosis system block diagram, white background. Raw water feed; pretreatment skid; high-pressure pump; parallel industrial membrane vessels; large atmospheric storage tank; repressurization delivery pump; household plumbing. Add a warning callout with the exact text 'Use PEX or CPVC Only'.

Reuse pathways diagram for reverse osmosis concentrate, clean white background. Center: RO system with brine output. Brine output branches to three destinations: a hot-water line via a zero-waste pump, a toilet cistern refill, and a landscape irrigation rain barrel. Add a side note with the exact text 'Keep Away From Sunlight'.

Service business model diagram for residential reverse osmosis maintenance, white background. Multiple installed customer RO units transmitting wireless telemetry data; a central computer monitoring dashboard; a technician driving a van dispatched based on alerts; and a recurring monthly subscription icon.

Final recap course-map infographic for the osmosis course, clean educational summary design on a white background. Five large connected blocks arranged from left to right. The blocks must contain the exact text labels: 'Foundations', 'System Design', 'Installation', 'Maintenance', and 'Advanced'. Connect the blocks with clean, thick progression arrows.
